query: LooseControlVideo complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Unpopular opinion? Control is a game that I regularly put down and walk away from : r/controlgame
results.content:
This is a common complaint around here, and most agree. At this point, it is what it is. It can be frustrating but don't let it ruin the whole game for you.

> Death in this game can happen extremely quickly. I regularly go from 100% to 5% health in a second (is it a single hit? who knows?). There's no life-regen, so basically, I can survive two hits.

Where do you find this happening? If it's in boss fights, yeah, they kill you real good-like. Outside of that, the floaty bois have an attack where they will lunge at you with metal. That and rockets are really the only thing that should do huge damage to you unless you're taking shotgun blasts to the face or letting the explody bois run up on you. [...] > There are so many lackluster collectables, memos, documents, etc. I really enjoyed finding and the pages within Alan Wake, but somehow it's either the number or the content of those within Control that makes these documents uninteresting distractions from the game.

I agree that some of the more redacted ones can be frustrating, but most of them give some pretty interesting background info on the objects, while some help with worldbuilding.

If you're not enjoying the game, that's fine. It's not for everyone. Finish it at your own pace.

[deleted]

•7y ago

> Probably because on console there's not enough buttons for that.

That's an excuse that people give for bad game design since over a decade ago. You can easily cycle through 4 weapons on a controller.

4 more replies [...] It's very very possible to find ways, even as a novice gamer, to build up your character to mitigate the risks posted above by the OP. This game is challenging, yes, and frustrating sometimes yes, overall the game feels very approachable.

Regarding checkpoints, they're EVERYWHERE. This game doesn't feel at all like it's got some crazy checkpointing going on. The control points are everywhere. keep playing and you unlock more and more.

To me, the OP just doesn't enjoy this style of campaign, and that's fair, but to call it out as poor game design and outdated control implementations, is not accurate. Remedy are VERY detail oriented, to a flaw, and they have absolutely thought through the why and how the gameplay mechanics were developed and implemented.

 Reply  Share

 below [...] # Why Do We Lose Control of Our Emotions?
## Kids Want to Know
33100 subscribers
23387 likes

### Description
3538012 views
Posted: 12 Mar 2017
Simple, easy-to-understand whiteboard animation to help early Elementary-aged children gain an understanding of the way their brains work to recognize and manage their emotions.  This is intended as a beginning resource to help children, parents, educators, and those who work with children to encourage mindfulness, empathy, and emotional regulation.

The Hand/Brain Model included in the video is a concept by Dr. Dan Siegel.  More information on "Flipping your Lid" and the Hand/Brain model can be found at www.drdansiegel.com.  You can check out a great video here:  

Other excellent resources include: [...] Other excellent resources include:

Zones of Regulation:  www.zonesofregulation.com
Mind UP: www.mindup.org

California Department of Education - Social Emotional Development:  

Developing Self Regulating Learners:  

Mindful Schools:  www.mindfulschools.org

Building Resilience in Children:  

Calm, Alert and Learning:
